title: Boat Rental in Moselle River 2026: Costs, Licences and Tips
html:
Exploring the Moselle River by boat is one of Europe's most scenic waterway experiences. From the historic town of Trier you can rent everything from small motorboats to crewed yachts, with prices starting around 150 EUR for a half-day. To find your ideal vessel, compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for boat rental and ownership.

Where to set off in Moselle River

The Moselle flows through Germany, Luxembourg and France, offering numerous launch points. Start at Port de Remich in Luxembourg, a modern marina with full facilities. In Germany, Marina Bernkastel-Kues sits amid vineyards, while Port de Thionville gives access to French wine regions. Koblenz Marina at the confluence with the Rhine is ideal for longer itineraries. Port de Saarburg provides a tranquil base near the Saar River junction. Each offers easy slipway access and fuel docks.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a variety of craft for the Moselle. Choose the right type for your party and budget.

  1. Motorboats, Best for couples or small families exploring short stretches, capacity 4, 6 guests, from 150 EUR for a half-day.
  2. Catamarans, ideal for groups wanting stability and space, capacity 8, 12 guests, from 400 EUR for a full day.
  3. Cabin cruisers, perfect for overnight trips with private berths, capacity 4, 6 guests, from 350 EUR per day.
  4. Crewed yachts, ensure a relaxing experience with a skipper, capacity up to 10 guests, from 900 EUR per day. See the Salty Sun Charters listing for a well-maintained Azimut example.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Moselle River?

A half-day motorboat rental starts at 150 EUR, and a full-day crewed yacht can exceed 1,200 EUR. Prices depend on boat size, season and duration.

  • Shared guided tour per person: from 50 EUR
  • Small motorboat (half-day, without fuel): 150, 250 EUR
  • Catamaran (full-day, self-drive): 400, 700 EUR
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, all-inclusive): 900, 1,500 EUR

For the most accurate rates, check boat rental Moselle River listings on BoatHire24, updated directly by owners.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Moselle River?

Yes, a valid inland waterways licence is required for self-drive boats over 15 hp on the Moselle. International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or German Sportbootführerschein Binnen is typically accepted. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking, so always check before you travel.

Always request a handover briefing and local charts; many hire companies provide a free lock guide for the 21 locks between Koblenz and Metz.

Peak season runs May to September, with July and August being busiest. The local wind, known as the 'Moselföhn', can cause sudden gusts in the valley, so inexperienced skippers should moor before midday in summer.

At a glance

  • Price range: 150, 1,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: May to September 2026
  • Typical trip length: half-day or full-day (up to 8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Required for self-drive over 15 hp

Questions about boat rental in Moselle River

Can I rent a boat without a licence on the Moselle?

Yes, some operators offer small electric boats with low horsepower that do not require a licence, and crewed charters always include a skipper.

What is the best length of trip from Bernkastel-Kues?

A half-day journey from Bernkastel-Kues to Traben-Trarbach covers about 12 nautical miles and takes three hours one-way with one lock.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a boat?

Most companies require the lead renter to be at least 21 years old with a valid driving licence and, where needed, a boating licence.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring provisions, but many boats have a galley with a fridge; check the equipment list on the BoatHire24 page.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned boater, the Moselle River offers a memorable mix of culture and nature. List your own vessel or book a trip through BoatHire24. Owners can reach thousands of potential renters and manage bookings easily.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Moselle River and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
